location: Carole L. Ellis Auditorium - Petaluma Campus
description: The Petaluma Cinema Series is a film education program that brings together SRJC film students, the campus community, and Sonoma County residents to engage in dialogue around classic, foreign, & independent films. All evenings include post-screening discussions.Pre-film program: 6pm  Film screening: 7pm (longer run times begin at 6:30pm)  Post-screening discussion until 10pm    For more information, visit: https://petalumafilmalliance.org/cinema-series/ (https://petalumafilmalliance.org/cinema-series/)

title: UPDATE: 1st Wednesday! Wednesday Night Walk start at STARBUCKS  on the East Side
description:
"SONOMA COUNTY OUTDOORS"
Wednesday Night Walk: Petaluma East Side Green Belt

* Want to walk for fitness and exercise?
* New to walking and want to build up stamina?
* Like a slower pace than our Tuesday Night Fitness Hike?
* Would walking with a committed group of walkers help you?

If you said yes to any of these, then the Wednesday Night Walk in Petaluma is for you. We start at STARBUCKS at the leghorn shopping center, and walk about 4-1/2 miles depending on weather conditions and the pace of the group that night.

DETAILS:

* Meetup time: 6:00pm (we walk at 6:10 sharp)
* Meetup location: STARBUCKS
* Distance: \~ 4.5 miles
* Duration: 75-80 minutes
* Pace: Easy/Moderate (about 2.5-3 mph or 20-24 minute miles)
* Difficulty: Easy (if you've walked 3-4 miles recently)
* Hills: Mostly flat
* Optional After-walk gathering at
* WHAT TO BRING:
* A good pair of walking shoes
* Water (you'll be glad you did)
* A fun attitude!

SO HOW FAST ARE WE WALKING?
The Wednesday Night Walk is about 2.5 - 3 mph (20-24 minute miles)
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike is about 3.5-4 mph (15-17 minute miles)

HOW DO YOU PARTICIPATE?
Sign up with Meetup and RSVP Today! This also gets you on our email list to make you aware of upcoming events, hikes and walks. You will not be spammed. If you are, let me know. Spamming will not be tolerated.

WHY DO YOU NEED TO RSVP?
It's important to RSVP for these walks. When all the people have arrived for a walk that have RSVP'd, we leave... it might mean we leave early. If you didn't RSVP we are not going to wait for you. Plus, it's just a nice courtesy to the organizer and other participants. Please take the time to RSVP.

QUESTIONS?
Email me with any questions by using the "Contact Us" link on the left hand side bar of this meetup group page.

Hope to see you on a walk or hike soon!

Gary
SCO Organizer

description: Join us Thursday night for indoor live music at Adobe Road Winery! Sip on award-winning wines while enjoying talented local musicians in a warm and inviting atmosphere. Choose from wines by the glass or bottle and discover new favorites. Reserve your spot on Tock and make it a memorable night of great music, fine wine, and good company! Rising star, Michelle Lambert , brings a world of experience when she steps up to the microphone. This Pop singer/songwriter has toured from coast to coast making waves all over the country from the Great American Music Hall in San Francisco to the Bridgestone Arena in Nashville. Michelle’s 2021 single, Come to Me , has caught the attention of the press and reached #1 on Indie Radio for 4 consecutive weeks.

title: Study Abroad in Costa Rica – Summer 2026 Application Deadline
description: SRJC students have the opportunity to spend July 5–26, 2026 studying yoga and wellness in beautiful Costa Rica while earning 6 units of college credit. For more information, visit SRJC's Study Abroad website! (https://study-abroad.santarosa.edu/costa-rica-summer-2026-information)

title: Music Bingo - TV Theme Songs & Movie Mix
Scott Street Gin Jam: 1st & 3rd Thursdays EVERY Month
description:
Join us every other Thursday for a Jam like no other!

🎶 Scott Street Gin Jam at Griffo Distillery! 🎶

Join us EVERY 1st & 3rd Thursday at Griffo Distillery for the Scott Street Gin Jam! The Griffo jam is unlike any other and we want YOU to be a part of it! Doors open at 5pm, just in time to grab a cocktail, a seat, or load in your gear before the jam starts at 6pm!

All ages, all skill levels, and all genres welcome! Whether you play, sing, or just want to kick back with a cocktail and soak in the http://vibes...this http://vibes...this vibes...this is the best way to spend your Thursday!

PLUS, we've got "8 dolla holla cocktails" ALL NIGHT LONG!

Musicians: Bring your own instrument and amp if you have a preferred setup. Bring your pedals, http://horns...anything http://horns...anything horns...anything you've got or hop on our house drum kit, grab the mic and let's rock and roll!

Everyone else: Come grab a seat and watch the magic unfold! Bring your energy, your friends, your moves and get ready to dance your way into the weekend!

Hungry?! We've got you covered! Every 3rd Thursday our resident Grill Master, Ryan pulls his rig out to the patio, fires up the coals, and throws down for our Community BBQ & Potluck! Bring a side to share, something to throw on the grill, or your generosity for the tip jar - 'cuz wait till you try his http://ribs...and http://ribs...and ribs...and don't worry we've got veggie stuff too!

Come on out to make some music, shake your hips, sip on Griffo's ever-rotating cocktail menu, and keep the jam alive! See you at Thursdays Griffo Distillery! 🎸🥁🎷🍸 #ScottStreetGinJam #LiveMusic #GriffoDistillery

As featured in The Bohemian & Argus Courrier - Thank You Don Lewis & David Templeton!!

Please RSVP for this FREE, All Ages Event!

title: Bennett Friedman Quartet
location: Newman Auditorium (Emeritus Hall) - Santa Rosa Campus
description: The Music Department of Santa Rosa Junior College presents a jazz concert by the Bennett Friedman Quartet on Thursday, April 2, at 7:30 PM in Newman Auditorium on the SRJC campus. Guitarist Randy Vincent, bassist Jeff Denson, and drummer Kendrick Freeman will be featured.  The quartet will perform "Take Five" by Paul Desmond, compositions of Bobby Hutcherson, Wayne Shorter, and others.  General admission is $15, and $10 for students and seniors. Parking on campus is $4. Proceeds from the concert benefit the SRJC Foundation Jazz Studies Fund.Visit SRJC's Music Department website here! (https://music.santarosa.edu/events)

title: Frosé Fridays
location: Adobe Road Tasting Room, 6 Petaluma Blvd. N - Suite A1 Petaluma, CA
description: With all this warm weather lately, we’ve got the perfect thing for you to cool off with at our Tasting Room in Downtown Petaluma. Our winemaker, Garrett Martin, has crafted a refreshing new Frosé (frozen rosé) made from our very own 2024 Sonoma County Rosé . We hope you love it! Join us every Friday to enjoy a glass of frosé or any of our other award-winning wines in the heart of Petaluma. Whether you’re exploring wine country, planning a weekend outing, or looking for the best frosé in Sonoma County, Adobe Road Winery is your go-to destination. Even better, Inside Track Wine Club members enjoy additional club member pricing as well. Our frosé is available on Fridays, while supplies last. Walk-ins welcome, reservations recommended. Call or text (707) 774-6699 or use Tock to reserve online.

title: Sonoma County Student Film Festival
location: Ellis Auditorium - Petaluma Campus
description: The Sonoma County Student Film Festival was initiated 18 years ago by the Student Government President, Amanda Swan, and launched at SRJC Petaluma. The Sonoma County Film Festival (SCSFF) encourages students to build academic and professional connections with their peers and community. The goal of the event is to connect and introduce student filmmakers within the community to our own Film & Media Arts programs here at SRJC, other filmmakers and film and media opportunities in the area. title: Red Hill - Afternoon Hike
description:
Mindfull Hikes
***Please be in good enough shape to hike at a moderate/fast steady pace (+/-2.5 mph) and comfortable with elevation changes.***

**Distance:** +/- 5 miles

This is one of my very favorite places on planet earth. So much to love about the experience: Panoramic vista of the Russian River and Willow Creek Valley; expansive views of the ocean; small redwood groves; hills to climb; rock outcroppings; footbridges and lush ferns. The land is magical.

**The hike:**
The trail starts on the other side of Highway 1 and we'll start with a climb up the graveled fire road to the Pomo Canyon trail. We'll continue on Pomo Canyon Trail through grasslands, developing views of the coastline and small groves of redwoods. After a few miles we'll take a right at the Red Hill trail and continue to the top of the hill. At the top of Red Hill (there is a picnic table) is our snack break and views of the mighty ocean to take in. We'll venture down Red Hill trail where it joins the Pomo Canyon trail again to take us back to the parking lot.
Please note: this hike isn't going down into the campground as we've done before on occasion.

**Restroom**: At Trailhead

**Parking:** Kortum Trail Parking Lot - Free with plenty of spaces

**Where to meet:** Kortum Trail Parking Lot at Shell Beach, Bodega Bay, CA

*Please be on time. We’ll start promptly at the start time listed.* Give yourself a little extra time to arrive and get situated so that you’re part of the opening circle as this is an essential part of our time together.

**What to bring:** Snack, water, hat, good hiking shoes, a joyful spirit and laughter. Please dress appropriately for the weather that day. Layers of clothing are always advisable.
**===================================**
**Reminders:**
Mindful hikes are intended to be a way to connect more fully with ourselves, each other and with the land. There will be two periods of silent walking throughout the hike to allow the senses to be fully alive and felt. There will also be time during the hike to visit and connect with each other. These times of talking and connecting are an invitation to bring mindfulness to the present moment. **It's a time to put away the cell phone and cameras to disengage from technology to be fully present with each other, our *senses and the land we are inhabiting.***

***If you're on the waitlist for the hike and want to attend please message Vickie a few days before the scheduled hike. Most hikes have people drop off at the last minute and it's possible there is an opening.***

**Dāna**
In the spirit of the Buddhist tradition mindful hikes are offered as a dāna based event. Rick Hansen says “generosity is a beautiful practice that opens and gladdens the heart, relaxes the contraction of ‘self’ and ripples out into the world to touch many people.” The dāna practice is **heart centered** and in the spirit of generosity. Venmo or cash is graciously accepted. Venmo account is: @vickieix8; last four digits 4714 of my phone # if asked.

**No-Show Policy:** Please cancel at least 24 hours before the start of the hike. Be mindful that there may be a waitlist. If your plans change and you're not coming, opening up a spot 4-5 days before the hike it's more likely someone will be able to attend than if you cancel at the last minute. You're considered a no-show if you cancel at the last minute. Members with 2 no-shows will be removed from the group.

description:
Bernice “bere” Espinoza’s performances blend languages, classes, joy, and resistance, all with the rhythm of real life. She brings everything she has to every performance and this Poetry Month edition is sure to challenge and thrill. 
Found Poets! is about the entertainment of the spirit through performance poetry. From our development program grads to nationally acclaimed headliners, Found Poets! is a challenging, committed poetry community dedicated to hosting a good night out.

Bernice Espinoza
Bernice “bere” Espinoza is a civil rights activist with a lifelong dedication to social justice. Her poetry has been featured in Palabra by Teatro Campesino and I Love Immigrants at San Jose City College.

Jordan Ranft
Jordan Ranft’s poetry has appeared in Carve Magazine, Frontier Poetry, Beaver Magazine, Bodega, Moon City Review, and his chapbook, Said The Worms (Wrong Publishing, 2023). In 2015, he placed third at the National Poetry Slam as a member of Team Berkeley.

Joseph Jason Santiago LaCour is a Poet, Artist and Emcee living in Santa Cruz. A parent of grown girls, he has true love in his life and he wants to share his art, his heart, with you.

title: Luis & N Burma at Annadel
description:
Go Wild Hikers
A beautiful hike to take in the wildflowers of spring. We'll start by going up Richardson to Lake Ilsanjo, take a break at the lake or the gazebo, then proceed to Luis and down N Burma to Channel trail. Best for individuals with at least a moderate level of fitness.

**Where We Meet/Important Directions**
Trione - Annadel State Park
**Do Not Use Your GPS! Follow These Direction.**
Montgomery Drive to Channel Drive to the Very (Dead) End! **Way past the pay kiosk!** There is a Large Parking Lot and the Warren Richardson Trail head. Much Farther than you think....Keep going until you see us. Give yourself enough time to arrive, pay park fee and use the bathroom. It often takes longer than you think.

**The Hike (Group Hike #338)**
Richardson, Luis, N Burma and Channel Trails - A loop hike
Distance - 6 miles/9.7 kilometers
Elevation change - 830 feet/253 meters
Difficulty - 6+ out of 10 - Moderately easy
Exposures - 30 % sun/70% shade - rocky, maybe muddy, poison oak, ticks/insects.
Must be sure footed (confident and competent)!
Bring plenty of water. At least 68 oz./2 liter.
Dogs - Are not allowed
Facilities at trail head - Shade, picnic tables and toilet.
Cost - $5 Cash.
Parking - $7 State Parks fee Or Free with your State Park Pass.
Remember to wear appropriate hiking shoes, bring plenty of water, a hat, a snack and layered clothing.
Please Do Not Use Any scented products the day of a group hike!
Of course, we will all look out for each other but ultimately, hikers are responsible for their own safety and well-being during hikes.

**After Hike Gathering?**
**Pascaline**
1021 Hahman Drive
Santa Rosa, CA 95405
707.303.7151
Open Daily: 8:00 am – 4:00 pm
Lunch Daily: 10:30 am – 2:30 pm

**When We Meet**
Sunday April 5, 2026
830 am - 1130 am
If your plans change after you sign up even if you're on the waitlist, please update your RSVP.
Do Not Attend if you are sick, running a fever, were exposed to or have tested positive for Covid-19 or any other contagion!
Hike may be changed, postponed or cancelled due to excessive heat, smoke/AQI, rain, illness or zombie apocalypses etc.. Check your email the night before for any possible changes.

**Notice to New Hikers Only**
**"When a New Hiker (never hiked with me before) doesn't show up (No Show) to their first hike with the group, they will be removed (not banned) from the group immediately."**

title: Join us for Guided Buddhist Meditation and time for questions
description:
Meditate with a Monk in Sonoma County
New and experienced meditators are welcome. We will start with direct guidance to begin the practice and allow time to experience the silence of meditation on your own. There will be time at the end for your questions regarding meditation practice.

You may stay on for a potluck meal at 11:00 am. Welcome to bring a dish to share.

This is followed by Sutta Study program from 1:00 to 2:30 pm.

We meet outdoors in the Dhammadharini Monastery Garden in the Sala Pavillion, as long as conducive weather lasts.

Come in person, or register at [here](https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2) to attend online

title: Mindfulness Sunday Meditation: Cultivating Consciousness
description:
Meditate with a Monk in Sonoma County
Join us for a transformative workshop on mindfulness meditation and cultivating consciousness. Dive deep into the teachings of Theravada Buddhism, focusing on insight meditation to develop a clear understanding of the nature of the mind and consciousness. This workshop will include guided meditation sessions to help you strengthen your meditation practice and cultivate mindfulness in your daily life. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced meditator, this workshop offers a welcoming space to explore the depths of your own consciousness and enhance your spiritual journey. Come together with like-minded individuals to deepen your practice and connect with the wisdom of Theravada Buddhism, and provide an opportunity to deepen mindfulness, wisdom, and inner stillness.
Our sessions are led by Bhikkhunis closely associated with our Dhammadharini Sangha. Check out the [recording from the past event](https://youtube.com/live/s07U2kzWoWk?feature=share) on our YouTube channel.
You’re also welcome to join us in person at Dhammadharini Monastery, in the peaceful setting of our outdoor Dana Sala Pavilion
Register for this event on Dhammadharini's website to receive the address:
[https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2](https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2)
Check out the sangha website at
[https://www.dhammadharini.net](https://www.dhammadharini.net/)

title: English Country Dance Sebastopol
description:
North Bay Contra Dance
English country dancing is done in long lines (like contra), circles, or in geometric sets (squares, triangles). Like our contra dances, there is live music and a caller. You can come by yourself or with friends, since we change partners after each dance. If you’ve ever done contra or square dancing, much of our dance vocabulary will be familiar to you since both of those styles evolved from English country dancing.

The dance is held the 1st and 3rd Sundays of each month, 2-4:30 pm, to live music, all levels of experience welcome; and occasional 5th Sundays for Advanced dancers. Admission is $15 for members of NBCDS and its affiliates, $8 for young adults ages 19-30, and $3 for youth ages 6 – 18.

title: Petaluma Contra Dance
description:
North Bay Contra Dance
Contra Dance is fun, quick to learn, and great exercise. Every dance night has a live band, caller, and a quick start class for first timers.
In contra dance, you will dance with your partner but also with other couples as you progress down the line. The figures for each dance are done in a repeated sequence after a brief walk through by the caller. Each dance may last 10 – 12 minutes and then we encourage you to switch partners as the night goes on.
What to wear: Comfortable shoes that will enable you to pivot and slide on the wood floor.
When to arrive: If this is your first time, come 30 minutes early to check in, join the quick start class, and get you ready for the first dance.
How hard to learn? Most newcomers will find that they can get dancing right away as the figures are quite simple.
Come as a group, couple, or single! Our dance is inclusive to all ages and genders.
Cost: $13 members, $15 non-members, $8 student or limited income.

title: Tuesday Night Fitness Hike: 5+ Fast Miles, Downtown Petaluma and Western Hills
description:
"SONOMA COUNTY OUTDOORS"
WARNING: THE TUESDAY NIGHT FITNESS HIKE IS AN AEROBIC, FAST-PACED URBAN HIKE! WE TARGET A PACE OF 16-MINUTES PER MILE WITH ONLY BRIEF STOPS (TO REGROUP AS NEEDED). OUR SUMMER ROUTE INCLUDES THE HILLS IN HELEN PUTNAM PARK (740 FT. ELEVATION GAIN), AND OUR WINTER ROUTE EXPLORES THE WATER TANKS (690 FT. ELEVATION GAIN). IF YOU FIND YOU ARE HAVING DIFFICULTY KEEPING UP WITH THE LEADERS IN THIS GROUP, PLEASE USE THE OPT-OUT POINT AT THE FIRST MILE OF THE HIKE! ANOTHER OPTION IS OUR WEDNESDAY NIGHT WALK FOR A MORE MODERATE PACE. THANK YOU FOR HELPING ME KEEP THIS HIKE AN AEROBIC WORKOUT!

NOTE:
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike is posted on the SCO Calendar every week. Steve Tubbs is shown as the host each week but he might, or might not, show up to the event. This is, more often than not, a small group of regular, fit walkers who show up on time every Tuesday night to enjoy this rigorous walk. We leave promptly at 6:10 pm from the Kentucky Street entrance to the LanMart Building. So, if you don't see Steve on the Tuesday night you are there, you might see him on the next one.

Tuesday Night Fitness Hike, 5+ miles fast paced urban hike:

This Fitness Hike includes multiple steep uphill portions in Helen Putnam Park (during Daylight Savings Time), and at the Petaluma Water Tanks (after Daylight Savings Time ends).

Tuesday Night Fitness Hike:

* Warning! This is a FAST hike... a real workout!
* 5+ miles
* Meetup time 6:00 pm (We hike at 6:10 sharp)
* Difficulty is STRENUOUS!
* 90 minutes

This is a fast, evening Fitness Hike that starts downtown outside (on the sidewalk) of the LanMart Building (Brixx Restaurant, Heebe Jeebe) on Kentucky Street. We head west to Helen Putnam Park (or the Petaluma Water Tanks when DST ends) for some beautiful vistas before returning to our starting point on Kentucky Street. This hike is generally the same route and is focused primarily on fitness.

WHAT TO BRING:

* A good pair of walking/hiking shoes
* Water (you'll be glad you did)
* A fun attitude!

WHAT ABOUT THE DARKNESS?
Bring a SMALL flashlight. The sidewalks can be treacherous in west Petaluma, and you'll want to see those crags, cracks and upheavals in the concrete. Reflective clothing is also recommended so that vehicles can see you!

SO HOW FAST ARE WE WALKING?
The Wednesday Night Walk is about 2.5 mph (22 - 23 minute miles)
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike nearly 3.5 - 4.0 mph (we target a 16 minute mile, with multiple hills!)

HOW DO YOU PARTICIPATE?
Sign up with Meetup and RSVP Today! This also gets you on our email list to make you aware of upcoming events, hikes and walks.

WHY DO YOU NEED TO RSVP?

Please take the time to RSVP YES when you plan on attending an event. It is equally important to keep your RSVP up to date. If you've RSVP'd YES for an event, we are going to wait for you until the departure time. We leave promptly at 6:10 PM, so if you arrive late you can join us along the route. We typically hike the same route for each hike, and our focus is on aerobic exercise.

QUESTIONS?
Email me with any questions by using the "Contact Us" link on the left hand side bar of this meetup group page.

Hope to see you on a walk or hike soon!

SCO Organizers

description:
The Mindful Stream (CA)
A session inspired by Eckhart Tolle's practices. Feel free to join as we get still and practise presence within this moment. We will discuss and observe the obstacles that, seemingly, block us from being present and bring awareness to our unconsciousness in a safe, inclusive and affirming way.

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teaching and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous.

See you there!

Please be aware that sessions will close if no members have joined after 10 minutes.

Emrys has been passionate about mindfulness since 2010 and since 2021 he has been organising free, weekly online meditation groups. For more information on his free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group, visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/).

title: Book Club - Hope in the Dark
location: Crooked Goat Brewing, 110 Howard Street, Petaluma, CA, 94952
description: Cool Petaluma Book Club Our book for April, Hope in the Dark by Rebecca Solnit, shows us why we should not give up hope, even though our future is uncertain. In fact, we should be committed to it in all its unknowability. PLEASE NOTE: There are 5 copies at Copperfield’s; ask for one at the counter. Let them know you’re with the Cool Petaluma Climate Book Club. Plus, get 10% off! #block-48fda9e02d14584b80b1 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; --tweak-text-block-radius: unset; --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; border-width: unset; border-style: unset; border-color: unset; background-color: unset;}#block-48fda9e02d14584b80b1 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; padding: unset;}#block-48fda9e02d14584b80b1 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } #block-48fda9e02d14584b80b1 { } RSVP Here #block-41ef93226c8a0444c843 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; } #block-41ef93226c8a0444c843 { --opacity: 100%; --translate-x: 0px; --translate-y: 0px; --rotation: 0deg; --scale-x: 100%; --scale-y: 100%; --skew-x: 0deg; --skew-y: 0deg; --origin-x: 50%; --origin-y: 50%; opacity: var(--opacity, 1); transform: translateX(var(--translate-x)) translateY(var(--translate-y)) rotate(var(--rotation)) scale(var(--scale-x), var(--scale-y)) skewX(var(--skew-x)) skewY(var(--skew-y)); transform-origin: var(--origin-x) var(--origin-y); } Hope in the Dark Solnit’s book is a manifesto for hope in place of the despair that engulfs many activists when their campaigns to make the world a better place or safeguard local values from globalized corporations do not bear immediate fruit. Solnit advocates for embracing the darkness of an uncertain future and campaigning from the perspective that previously unforeseen changes are always possible. In this book, she seeks to safeguard against the cultural amnesia in which people forget that previously unthinkable events changed history, such as obtaining suffrage for women after millennia of patriarchy. In addition, she emphasizes that no easy cause-and-effect relationship exists between activism and seeing changes realized. Instead, the path to change twists and turns, with many defeats as well as small victories. #block-5ce66d8084f2469abf99 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; --tweak-text-block-radius: unset; --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; border-width: unset; border-style: unset; border-color: unset; background-color: unset;}#block-5ce66d8084f2469abf99 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; padding: unset;}#block-5ce66d8084f2469abf99 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } #block-5ce66d8084f2469abf99 { }   [ { "id": "04388466-7135-4ccc-9916-e993de72b63e" } ] Get your copy & start reading: Petaluma Library GoodReads Copperfield’s Books - Tell them you are with the Cool Petaluma Book Club for 10% discount! #block-3fc3e09189c20756044f { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; border-radius: var(--tweak-text-block-radius); --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; border-width: unset; border-style: unset; border-color: unset; box-sizing: border-box;height: 100%;background-color: var(--tweak-text-block-background-color);}#block-3fc3e09189c20756044f .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; padding: var(--tweak-text-block-padding);}#block-3fc3e09189c20756044f { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } #block-3fc3e09189c20756044f { }

title: Mindfulness Tuesday Meditation: Cultivating Consciousness
description:
Meditate with a Monk in Sonoma County
Join us for a transformative workshop on mindfulness meditation and cultivating consciousness. Dive deep into the teachings of Theravada Buddhism, focusing on insight meditation to develop a clear understanding of the nature of the mind and consciousness. This workshop will include guided meditation sessions to help you strengthen your meditation practice and cultivate mindfulness in your daily life. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced meditator, this workshop offers a welcoming space to explore the depths of your own consciousness and enhance your spiritual journey. Come together with like-minded individuals to deepen your practice and connect with the wisdom of Theravada Buddhism.
Our sessions are led by Bhikkhunis closely associated with our Dhammadharini Sangha. Check out Dhammadharini Sangha at [https://www.dhammadharini.net/](https://www.dhammadharini.net/)

title: Free Estate Planning Webinar
location: https://mackaydressler.com/workshops
description:
Details


Strategies to Manage Incapacity, Family Dynamics, and Financial Risks

Creating your estate plan, whether it’s a living trust or a will, can be a daunting task. Estate planning addresses issues that are difficult to think about, such as death, incapacity, finances, and family dynamics. It can be easy to procrastinate, even though these issues may weigh heavily on your mind.

We invite you to attend one of our upcoming free webinars so you can take the first step toward having peace of mind by creating (or updating) your estate plan. This is an educational webinar, where we highlight some of the concerns that many families face and how an estate plan can help. The attorneys also break down how wills and trusts differ, and some of the common estate planning pitfalls and how to avoid them.

Our goal is to provide you with the education you need to protect yourself and your family, and leave a legacy of peace. If you would like to set up a meeting to discuss your estate planning needs further, we offer all participants who complete the webinar a free, no obligation personal consultation with one of the attorneys.

At this webinar, we will discuss several common issues and concerns, including:

  * The advantages and disadvantages of wills, living trusts, and probate
  * Protecting your children’s inheritance from their future ex-spouses, lawsuits, and other claims
  * Most living trusts fail; if you already have a trust, find out what you can do to ensure that it will protect you and your loved ones
  * How to preserve your nest egg for your family if you find yourself needing Long Term Care
  * How to stay out of court and avoid the cost, time, and stress that a conservatorship can bring if you become incapacitated.

Attend a webinar and receive a FREE no-obligation consultation with an Attorney to answer any questions about setting up your estate plan ($550 value).

title: Figure Drawing Night at Suite G Studio!
description:
Petaluma Figure Drawing Meetup Group
Join us for an evening of creativity and inspiration at the Petaluma Figure Drawing Meetup Group's Figure Drawing Night at Suite G Studio! Whether you're a seasoned artist or just starting out, this event is the perfect opportunity to hone your skills, meet fellow artists, and immerse yourself in the world of figurative painting. Our life drawing sessions are open to artists and art lovers of all levels, so don't be shy to join us and express your creativity on paper. We provide a supportive and encouraging environment where everyone can feel comfortable exploring their artistic talents.

[BECOME A MEMBER](https://www.suitegstudio.com/artist-resources) of Suite G Studio for $40/month. Or RSVP by sending $25 to @suiteg on Venmo or email suitegstudio620@gmail.com

Be prepared for an evening filled with sketching, painting, and drawing as we focus on capturing the human form in all its beauty and complexity. This event is a great opportunity to practice your figure drawing techniques and unleash your artistic potential. Whether you prefer using pencils, charcoal, pastels, or paint, there's something for everyone at our Figure Drawing Night. Come and be a part of our community of artists dedicated to celebrating and expressing the beauty of life through art.

title: Wednesday Night Walk: Petaluma Downtown & Riverfront
description:
"SONOMA COUNTY OUTDOORS"
Wednesday Night Walk: Petaluma Downtown and Riverfront

* Want to walk for fitness and exercise?
* New to walking and want to build up stamina?
* Like a slower pace than our Tuesday Night Fitness Hike?
* Would walking with a committed group of walkers help you?

If you said yes to any of these, then the Wednesday Night Walk in Petaluma is for you. We start at Aqus Cafe and take in the West Side of town, including some of Downtown, the Waterfront and a few of the Historic home of old Petaluma. We walk about 3-1/2 to 4-1/2 miles depending on weather conditions and the pace of the group that night.

Afterward if you have the time, join us at Aqus Cafe for a bite to eat and some liquid refreshment before heading home. Aqus Cafe is a true gem of a gathering place, restaurant, coffee house, and live music venue. But it's the people on either side of the counter that make this place so special.

DETAILS:

* Meetup time: 6:00pm (we hike at 6:10 sharp)
* Meetup location: Aqus Cafe
* Distance: 3.5 to 4-1/2 miles
* Duration: 75-80 minutes
* Pace: Easy/Moderate (about 2.5-3 mph or 20-24 minute miles)
* Difficulty: Easy (if you've walked 3-4 miles recently)
* Hills: Mostly flat with a few small and easy to climb hills
* Optional After-Hike Gathering at Aqus Cafe

WHAT TO BRING:

* A good pair of walking shoes
* Water (you'll be glad you did)
* A fun attitude!

SO HOW FAST ARE WE WALKING?
The Wednesday Night Walk is about 2.5 - 3 mph (20-24 minute miles)
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike is about 3.5-4 mph (15-17 minute miles)

HOW DO YOU PARTICIPATE?
Sign up with Meetup and RSVP Today! This also gets you on our email list to make you aware of upcoming events, hikes and walks. You will not be spammed. If you are, let me know. Spamming will not be tolerated.

WHY DO YOU NEED TO RSVP?
It's important to RSVP for these walks. When all the people have arrived for a walk that have RSVP'd, we leave... it might mean we leave early. If you didn't RSVP we are not going to wait for you. Plus, it's just a nice courtesy to the organizer and other participants. Please take the time to RSVP.

QUESTIONS?
Email me with any questions by using the "Contact Us" link on the left hand side bar of this meetup group page.

Hope to see you on a walk or hike soon!

Gary
SCO Organizer

title: Afternoon Hike at Annadel - Parktrail
description:
Mindfull Hikes
***Please be in good enough shape to hike at a moderate/fast steady pace (+/-2.5 mph) and comfortable with elevation changes.***

Celebrating the end of the day with a hike to Lake Ilsanjo.

**Distance:** +/- 5 miles total
**The hike:** A lollipop hike that includes Canyon, Lake (around the lake) and Spring Trails. There are some hills to climb on this hike.

**Parking:** Street parking on Parktrail Drive

**Where to Meet:** Intersection of Parktrail Drive and Comfrey Place on the sidewalk.

*Please be on time. We’ll start promptly at the start time listed.* Give yourself a little extra time to arrive and get situated so that you’re part of the opening circle as this is an essential part of our time together.

**What to bring:** Snack, water, hat, good hiking shoes, a joyful spirit and laughter. Please dress appropriately for the weather that day. Layers of clothing are always advisable.
**===================================**
**Reminders:**
Mindful hikes are intended to be a way to connect more fully with ourselves, each other and with the land. There will be two periods of silent walking throughout the hike to allow the senses to be fully alive and felt. There will also be time during the hike to visit and connect with each other. These times of talking and connecting are an invitation to bring mindfulness to the present moment. **It's a time to put away the cell phone and cameras to disengage from technology to be fully present with each other, our *senses and the land we are inhabiting.***

***If you're on the waitlist for the hike and want to attend please message Vickie a few days before the scheduled hike. Most hikes have people drop off at the last minute and it's possible there is an opening.***

**Dāna**
In the spirit of the Buddhist tradition mindful hikes are offered as a dāna based event. Rick Hansen says “generosity is a beautiful practice that opens and gladdens the heart, relaxes the contraction of ‘self’ and ripples out into the world to touch many people.” The dāna practice is **heart centered** and in the spirit of generosity. Venmo or cash is graciously accepted. Venmo account is: @vickieix8; last four digits 4714 of my phone # if asked.

**No-Show Policy:** Please cancel at least 24 hours before the start of the hike. Be mindful that there may be a waitlist. If your plans change and you're not coming, opening up a spot 4-5 days before the hike it's more likely someone will be able to attend than if you cancel at the last minute. You're considered a no-show if you cancel at the last minute. Members with 2 no-shows are removed from the group.

title: Happy Hour Crafts
description:
The Rebel Craft Collective
Wine + tasty snacks + your latest project and a friendly group of likeminded crafters... what better way to unwind? Come join us!

This is a relaxed, drop-in-friendly event. Come when you can, leave when you must. Newcomers and occasional-comers are always welcome!

**What do we mean by “crafts”?**
Whatever “craft” means to you! And whatever is portable enough to bring to a wine bar. Knitting, crochet, and other fiber arts are most common, but we have also warmly welcomed people who sketch, color, embroider, bead, and more. If your craft isn’t portable, we will happily ooh and aah over photos of your work-in-progress, or chat about your plans for your next project.

**Health & Safety**
Masks are very welcome, and nobody will pressure you to eat or drink. If the weather is nice, we will try to sit outside. If you aren’t eating/drinking during the event, please consider ordering some of La Dolce Vita’s delicious food to-go, in order to help support our gracious, long-time venue.

**Parking**
Parking in downtown Petaluma is free, but street parking has a strictly enforced 2 hour limit until 6pm. To park for longer, try the nearest garage:

* The Second Street Garage entrance is located on C Street between First and Second Streets. It's all day parking on every level and a short walk to La Dolce Vita.

**Ages**
It's a wine bar, so this event is for adult crafters (though you're certainly not required to drink wine!). We’re okay with babes-in-arms if you are, but this is not the right setting for wee crafters who are moving about on their own.

title: Fernando Fidanza - Italian folk for "The Sound of California"
location: The Big Easy, 128 American Alley, Petaluma, CA, 94952, United States
description:
“The Sound of California”, a tribute to the art, the culture, the history, the folklore and the cuisine of your beautiful state.
The project will become a book, a documentary, a Youtube and Instagram channel.
Fernando Fidanza, born in Rome in 1976, started to play guitar when he was 8 years old , with his brother’s electric guitar, inspired by U2. At 12, in 1988, the encounter that changed his life. He discovered heavy metal, mostly with three albums, …And Justice For All by Metallica, South Of Heaven by Slayer and Live After Death by Iron Maiden. From that moment guitar and music became his passions, his dreams, his life. Thanks to his parents’ mindset he grew up believing that travels and art are the most beautiful and important things in life. He travelled a lot with his music, and now, in collaboration with his brother Luca, has decided to discover The Sound of The World. So he changes country every two or three months, playing his songs and writing books and shoooting documentaries about the countries he visits and the people he meets. Now, it is California’s turn.
If you want to follow my journey, here is my Instagram: resonance_thesoundof

title: Inner Body Guided-Meditation
description:
The Mindful Stream (CA)
"Awareness of the inner body takes you beyond the mind." - Eckhart Tolle

In this mediation session we will bring our attention here: to the inner body where we practise presence by focusing on our physical sensations.

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. title: Jenner Headlands
description:
Go Wild Hikers
A good day to look for wildflowers. Jenner Headlands is a spectacular 5,630-acre mosaic of redwood and Douglas fir forests, oak woodland, chaparral, and coastal prairie overlooking the Pacific Ocean. Coastal prairie is rare and only found along the Pacific Coast of California and southern Oregon. These areas support a rich assemblage of native plants and are one of the most diverse types of grasslands in North America. Many rare and endangered species inhabit the property including the northern spotted owl, peregrine falcon, and red tree vole. Deer, bobcat, coyote, mountain lion and steelhead trout are also found on the property. A challenging hike best for individuals and dogs with at least a moderate level of fitness.

**Where We Meet/Important Directions**
Jenner Headlands Preserve
Hwy 1 Jenner, Ca
**Get there Early! Carpool if you can - This is a very Popular Park!**
Give yourself plenty of time to arrive and use restroom. It often takes longer than you think.

**Six things to remember when hiking near cattle**
1- Properly latch any gates you pass through on trails.
2- Always keep dogs on leash. Do not let pets chase or harass livestock.
Cows cannot distinguish the difference between a coyote and a dog.
3- If cattle are blocking the trail, approach them slowly, speak normally, and allow them to move away. If they don’t move away on their own, provide a wide berth by walking around them off-trail, if necessary.
4- If you see a stray calf, leave it alone; the mother is often feeding or watering nearby and will return. Do not get between a mother cow and young calves.
5- Give space to any cow that shows signs of aggression. This may mean giving them lots of space and walking off-trail if necessary.
6- If you encounter a cow that is acting in a threatening manner, or appears to be injured, sick or dead, please note the location, the color of the animal, the ear tag number, and report it to the park.
https://parks.sonomacounty.ca.gov/Learn/Blog/Articles/Sharing-Sonoma-County-trails-with-cows/

**The Hike (Group Hike #339)**
Sea to Sky and Raptor Ridge trails - A loop hike
Length - +/-5 miles/8.1 kilometers
Elevation change - +/-950 feet/290 meters
Difficulty - 7+/10 - some steep & rocky sections and a seasonal creek crossing. Must be sure footed (confident and competent)! Bring trekking poles.
Exposure - 99% sun/1% shade - steep, rocky, maybe mud, ticks/insects, poison oak and cows.
Bring plenty of water! At least 68 oz./2 liters **\*Think Exposure\***.
Dogs - Yes, well behaved fit dogs on a leash are welcome.
Facilities at trail head - Picnic tables and restrooms.
Cost - $5 Cash.
Parking - Free but only 30 parking spaces! Overflow parking on Hwy 1.
**Get there Early! Carpool if you can - This is a very Popular Park!**
Remember to wear appropriate hiking shoes, bring plenty of water, for your dog too, a hat, snack/lunch and layered clothing.
Please Do Not Use Any scented products the day of a group hike!
Of course, we will all look out for each other but ultimately, hikers are responsible for their own safety and well-being during hikes.

**After Hike Gathering**
Café Aquatica
(One of our favorites
and dogs are allowed)
10439 CA-1, Jenner, CA 95450
https://www.cafeaquaticajenner.com/#/

**When We Meet**
Saturday April 11, 2026
8:30 am - 11:30 pm
**Get there Early! Carpool if you can - This is a very Popular Park!**
**If your plans change after you sign up even if you're on the waitlist, please update your RSVP.**
Do Not Attend if you are sick, running a fever, were exposed to or have tested positive for Covid-19 or any other contagion!
Hike may be changed, rescheduled or cancelled in case of excessive heat, rain, smoke/AQI, illness, zombie apocalypse etc... Check your email the night before for any possible changes.

**Notice to New Hikers Only**
***"When a New Hiker (never hiked with me before) doesn't show up (No Show) to their first hike with the group, they will be removed (not banned) from the group immediately."***

title: English Country Dance Marin
description:
North Bay Contra Dance
English country dancing is done in long lines (like contra), circles, or in geometric sets (squares, triangles). Like our contra dances, there is live music and a caller. You can come by yourself or with friends, since we change partners after each dance. If you’ve ever done contra or square dancing, much of our dance vocabulary will be familiar to you since both of those styles evolved from English country dancing.

Admission is $15 for members of NBCDS and its affiliates.

title: San Rafael Contra Dance
description:
North Bay Contra Dance
Contra Dance is fun, quick to learn, and great exercise. Every dance night has a live band, caller, and a quick start class for first timers.
In contra dance, you will dance with your partner but also with other couples as you progress down the line. The figures for each dance are done in a repeated sequence after a brief walk through by the caller. Each dance may last 10 – 12 minutes and then we encourage you to switch partners as the night goes on.
What to wear: Comfortable shoes that will enable you to pivot and slide on the wood floor.
When to arrive: If this is your first time, come 30 minutes early to check in, join the quick start class, and get you ready for the first dance.
How hard to learn? Most newcomers will find that they can get dancing right away as the figures are quite simple.
Come as a group, couple, or single! Our dance is inclusive to all ages and genders.
Cost: *Free to first-time dancers*. $13 members, $15 non-members, $8 student or limited income.

description:

You may stay on for a potluck meal at 11:00 am. Welcome to bring a dish to share.

We meet outdoors in the Dhammadharini Monastery Garden in the Sala Pavillion, as long as conducive weather lasts.

title: Mindfulness Sunday Meditation: Cultivating Consciousness
description:
Meditate with a Monk in Sonoma County
Join us for a transformative workshop on mindfulness meditation and cultivating consciousness. Dive deep into the teachings of Theravada Buddhism, focusing on insight meditation to develop a clear understanding of the nature of the mind and consciousness. This workshop will include guided meditation sessions to help you strengthen your meditation practice and cultivate mindfulness in your daily life. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced meditator, this workshop offers a welcoming space to explore the depths of your own consciousness and enhance your spiritual journey. Come together with like-minded individuals to deepen your practice and connect with the wisdom of Theravada Buddhism, and provide an opportunity to deepen mindfulness, wisdom, and inner stillness.
Our sessions are led by Bhikkhunis closely associated with our Dhammadharini Sangha. Check out the [recording from the past event](https://youtube.com/live/s07U2kzWoWk?feature=share) on our YouTube channel.
You’re also welcome to join us in person at Dhammadharini Monastery, in the peaceful setting of our outdoor Dana Sala Pavilion
Register for this event on Dhammadharini's website to receive the address:
[https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2](https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2)
Check out the sangha website at
[https://www.dhammadharini.net](https://www.dhammadharini.net/)

title: In Conversation with Inez Storer Moderated by Donna Seager
location: 230 Lakeville Street, Petaluma, CA 94952
description: In Conversation with Inez Storer Moderated by Donna Seager Sunday, April 12, 4:00-5:00pm $15 members; $20 non-members An afternoon conversation about Inez’s approach to artmaking, her family history and the stories that inform her art. There will be time for questions. Storer lives in Inverness, California, and has a studio in Point Reyes Station. Born in Santa Monica in 1933 to a Catholic father and Jewish mother, she spent time on Hollywood movie sets where her father worked as an art director. Influenced by stage design, her work often creates a sense of “theatrical space” and reflects her ancestry. Shortly before her death, her mother revealed that she had fled Germany to escape Nazism. Storer later learned of cousins who escaped on the Kindertransport. The frustration of being kept from the truth has infused her work with a distinct angst. Her work is held in many collections including the Jewish Museum in New York, Manetti Shrem Museum of Art at UC Davis, Oakland Museum of Art , the de Young Museum, San José Museum of Art, Monterey Museum of Art, Missoula Museum of Art, National Museum of American Jewish History, and the National Museum of Women in the Arts, Washington, DC. #block-887d4e75ff4a9e1a9a21 { --tweak-text-block-radius: 0px 0px 0px 0px; --tweak-text-block-radius: unset; --stroke-style: none;--stroke-thickness: 6px; border-width: unset; border-style: unset; border-color: unset; background-color: unset;}#block-887d4e75ff4a9e1a9a21 .sqs-html-content { --tweak-text-block-padding: 6% 6% 6% 6%; padding: unset;}#block-887d4e75ff4a9e1a9a21 { mix-blend-mode: var(--tweak-text-block-blend ); } #block-887d4e75ff4a9e1a9a21 { } Get tickets #block-1561e173823053783d43 {--sqs-block-content-flex: 0; }

title: "Monet & Venice" Visiting Artist Lecture with Professor Heidi Saleh
location: Doyle Library, Room 146 - Santa Rosa Campus
description: Please join Art History Professor Heidi Saleh for an upcoming lecture examining Claude Monet's celebrated 1908 Venetian campaign.  Designed as a companion to the Monet and Venice exhibition currently on view (3/21/26 – 7/26/26) at the de Young Museum in San Francisco, this talk will explore the historical and stylistic context of Monet's time in the Floating City. We will analyze his approach to seriality, his technique for capturing light on water, and how these canvases fit into the broader trajectory of late Impressionism.  Whether you have already visited the de Young exhibition or are planning a trip, this lecture will provide a deeper framework for appreciating and engaging with these works.  For more information, visit SRJC's Agrella Art Gallery website! (https://art-gallery.santarosa.edu/exhibition/visiting-artist-lecture-series-spring-2026)  Also on Zoom: https://santarosa-edu.zoom.us/j/85748104401 (https://santarosa-edu.zoom.us/j/85748104401)   Meeting ID: 857 4810 440

title: Tuesday Night Fitness Hike: 5+ Fast Miles, Downtown Petaluma and Western Hills
description:
"SONOMA COUNTY OUTDOORS"
WARNING: THE TUESDAY NIGHT FITNESS HIKE IS AN AEROBIC, FAST-PACED URBAN HIKE! WE TARGET A PACE OF 16-MINUTES PER MILE WITH ONLY BRIEF STOPS (TO REGROUP AS NEEDED). OUR SUMMER ROUTE INCLUDES THE HILLS IN HELEN PUTNAM PARK (740 FT. ELEVATION GAIN), AND OUR WINTER ROUTE EXPLORES THE WATER TANKS (690 FT. ELEVATION GAIN). IF YOU FIND YOU ARE HAVING DIFFICULTY KEEPING UP WITH THE LEADERS IN THIS GROUP, PLEASE USE THE OPT-OUT POINT AT THE FIRST MILE OF THE HIKE! ANOTHER OPTION IS OUR WEDNESDAY NIGHT WALK FOR A MORE MODERATE PACE. THANK YOU FOR HELPING ME KEEP THIS HIKE AN AEROBIC WORKOUT!

NOTE:
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike is posted on the SCO Calendar every week. Steve Tubbs is shown as the host each week but he might, or might not, show up to the event. This is, more often than not, a small group of regular, fit walkers who show up on time every Tuesday night to enjoy this rigorous walk. We leave promptly at 6:10 pm from the Kentucky Street entrance to the LanMart Building. So, if you don't see Steve on the Tuesday night you are there, you might see him on the next one.

Tuesday Night Fitness Hike, 5+ miles fast paced urban hike:

This Fitness Hike includes multiple steep uphill portions in Helen Putnam Park (during Daylight Savings Time), and at the Petaluma Water Tanks (after Daylight Savings Time ends).

Tuesday Night Fitness Hike:

* Warning! This is a FAST hike... a real workout!
* 5+ miles
* Meetup time 6:00 pm (We hike at 6:10 sharp)
* Difficulty is STRENUOUS!
* 90 minutes

This is a fast, evening Fitness Hike that starts downtown outside (on the sidewalk) of the LanMart Building (Brixx Restaurant, Heebe Jeebe) on Kentucky Street. We head west to Helen Putnam Park (or the Petaluma Water Tanks when DST ends) for some beautiful vistas before returning to our starting point on Kentucky Street. This hike is generally the same route and is focused primarily on fitness.

WHAT TO BRING:

* A good pair of walking/hiking shoes
* Water (you'll be glad you did)
* A fun attitude!

WHAT ABOUT THE DARKNESS?
Bring a SMALL flashlight. The sidewalks can be treacherous in west Petaluma, and you'll want to see those crags, cracks and upheavals in the concrete. Reflective clothing is also recommended so that vehicles can see you!

SO HOW FAST ARE WE WALKING?
The Wednesday Night Walk is about 2.5 mph (22 - 23 minute miles)
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike nearly 3.5 - 4.0 mph (we target a 16 minute mile, with multiple hills!)

HOW DO YOU PARTICIPATE?
Sign up with Meetup and RSVP Today! This also gets you on our email list to make you aware of upcoming events, hikes and walks.

WHY DO YOU NEED TO RSVP?

Please take the time to RSVP YES when you plan on attending an event. It is equally important to keep your RSVP up to date. If you've RSVP'd YES for an event, we are going to wait for you until the departure time. We leave promptly at 6:10 PM, so if you arrive late you can join us along the route. We typically hike the same route for each hike, and our focus is on aerobic exercise.

QUESTIONS?
Email me with any questions by using the "Contact Us" link on the left hand side bar of this meetup group page.

Hope to see you on a walk or hike soon!

SCO Organizers

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teaching and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous.

Emrys has been passionate about mindfulness since 2010 and since 2021 he has been organising free, weekly online meditation groups. For more information on his free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group, visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/).

title: Grandma Gatewood's Walk by Ben Montgomery
description:
Petaluma Book (And Brew) Club
Grandma Gatewood, as the reporters called her, became the first woman to hike the entire Appalachian Trail alone, as well as the first person—man or woman—to walk it twice and three times. Gatewood became a hiking celebrity and appeared on TV and in the pages of Sports Illustrated. The public attention she brought to the little-known footpath was unprecedented. Her vocal criticism of the lousy, difficult stretches led to bolstered maintenance, and very likely saved the trail from extinction.

If you are an audio book or an e-reader this book is on Hoopla.

title: Mindfulness Tuesday Meditation: Cultivating Consciousness
description:
Meditate with a Monk in Sonoma County
Join us for a transformative workshop on mindfulness meditation and cultivating consciousness. Dive deep into the teachings of Theravada Buddhism, focusing on insight meditation to develop a clear understanding of the nature of the mind and consciousness. This workshop will include guided meditation sessions to help you strengthen your meditation practice and cultivate mindfulness in your daily life. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced meditator, this workshop offers a welcoming space to explore the depths of your own consciousness and enhance your spiritual journey. Come together with like-minded individuals to deepen your practice and connect with the wisdom of Theravada Buddhism.
Our sessions are led by Bhikkhunis closely associated with our Dhammadharini Sangha. Check out Dhammadharini Sangha at [https://www.dhammadharini.net/](https://www.dhammadharini.net/)
Register for this event on Dhammadharini's website:
[https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/tuesday-evening-meditation-dhamma-talk-2](https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/tuesday-evening-meditation-dhamma-talk-2)

title: Wednesday Night Walk: Petaluma Downtown & Riverfront
description:
"SONOMA COUNTY OUTDOORS"
Wednesday Night Walk: Petaluma Downtown and Riverfront

* Want to walk for fitness and exercise?
* New to walking and want to build up stamina?
* Like a slower pace than our Tuesday Night Fitness Hike?
* Would walking with a committed group of walkers help you?

If you said yes to any of these, then the Wednesday Night Walk in Petaluma is for you. We start at Aqus Cafe and take in the West Side of town, including some of Downtown, the Waterfront and a few of the Historic home of old Petaluma. We walk about 3-1/2 to 4-1/2 miles depending on weather conditions and the pace of the group that night.

Afterward if you have the time, join us at Aqus Cafe for a bite to eat and some liquid refreshment before heading home. Aqus Cafe is a true gem of a gathering place, restaurant, coffee house, and live music venue. But it's the people on either side of the counter that make this place so special.

DETAILS:

* Meetup time: 6:00pm (we hike at 6:10 sharp)
* Meetup location: Aqus Cafe
* Distance: 3.5 to 4-1/2 miles
* Duration: 75-80 minutes
* Pace: Easy/Moderate (about 2.5-3 mph or 20-24 minute miles)
* Difficulty: Easy (if you've walked 3-4 miles recently)
* Hills: Mostly flat with a few small and easy to climb hills
* Optional After-Hike Gathering at Aqus Cafe

WHAT TO BRING:

* A good pair of walking shoes
* Water (you'll be glad you did)
* A fun attitude!

SO HOW FAST ARE WE WALKING?
The Wednesday Night Walk is about 2.5 - 3 mph (20-24 minute miles)
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike is about 3.5-4 mph (15-17 minute miles)

HOW DO YOU PARTICIPATE?
Sign up with Meetup and RSVP Today! This also gets you on our email list to make you aware of upcoming events, hikes and walks. You will not be spammed. If you are, let me know. Spamming will not be tolerated.

WHY DO YOU NEED TO RSVP?
It's important to RSVP for these walks. When all the people have arrived for a walk that have RSVP'd, we leave... it might mean we leave early. If you didn't RSVP we are not going to wait for you. Plus, it's just a nice courtesy to the organizer and other participants. Please take the time to RSVP.

QUESTIONS?
Email me with any questions by using the "Contact Us" link on the left hand side bar of this meetup group page.

Hope to see you on a walk or hike soon!

Gary
SCO Organizer

title: Scott Street Gin Jam: 1st & 3rd Thursdays EVERY Month
description:
Join us every other Thursday for a Jam like no other!

🎶 Scott Street Gin Jam at Griffo Distillery! 🎶

Join us EVERY 1st & 3rd Thursday at Griffo Distillery for the Scott Street Gin Jam! The Griffo jam is unlike any other and we want YOU to be a part of it! Doors open at 5pm, just in time to grab a cocktail, a seat, or load in your gear before the jam starts at 6pm!

All ages, all skill levels, and all genres welcome! Whether you play, sing, or just want to kick back with a cocktail and soak in the http://vibes...this http://vibes...this vibes...this is the best way to spend your Thursday!

PLUS, we've got "8 dolla holla cocktails" ALL NIGHT LONG!

Musicians: Bring your own instrument and amp if you have a preferred setup. Bring your pedals, http://horns...anything http://horns...anything horns...anything you've got or hop on our house drum kit, grab the mic and let's rock and roll!

Everyone else: Come grab a seat and watch the magic unfold! Bring your energy, your friends, your moves and get ready to dance your way into the weekend!

Hungry?! We've got you covered! Every 3rd Thursday our resident Grill Master, Ryan pulls his rig out to the patio, fires up the coals, and throws down for our Community BBQ & Potluck! Bring a side to share, something to throw on the grill, or your generosity for the tip jar - 'cuz wait till you try his http://ribs...and http://ribs...and ribs...and don't worry we've got veggie stuff too!

Come on out to make some music, shake your hips, sip on Griffo's ever-rotating cocktail menu, and keep the jam alive! See you at Thursdays Griffo Distillery! 🎸🥁🎷🍸 #ScottStreetGinJam #LiveMusic #GriffoDistillery

As featured in The Bohemian & Argus Courrier - Thank You Don Lewis & David Templeton!!

title: Inner Body Guided-Meditation
description:
The Mindful Stream (CA)
"Awareness of the inner body takes you beyond the mind." - Eckhart Tolle

In this mediation session we will bring our attention here: to the inner body where we practise presence by focusing on our physical sensations.

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. They will last around 20 minutes and cameras and microphones will be disabled. (Depending on the situation, a free discussion may be held at the end with mics and cameras allowed.)

Sessions are suitable for newcomers, those experienced in Tolle's teachings and anyone wishing to remain completely anonymous. They will last around 20 minutes and cameras and microphones will be disabled. (Depending on the situation, a free discussion may be held at the end with mics and cameras allowed.)

See you there!

Emrys also organises a free Work of Byron Katie online mediation group. Visit [this Meetup Group link](https://www.meetup.com/thework-runsfree/) for more information.

title: Headwaters to Headwaters Hike & BBQ - Sugarloaf Fundraising Event
description:
Mindfull Hikes
\*\*\*\* 3/28/2026; event is FULL; closing RSVPs\*\*\*\*\*

Let's support our local state park and hike this amazing adventure together.

A fundraiser for Sugarloaf Ridge State Park and Mayacamas hiking adventure. Join Sonoma Ecology Center and Team Sugarloaf for an unforgettable journey through the heart of Sonoma County's most pristine and breathtaking landscapes, in our exclusive fundraising hike on **Saturday, April 18, 2026** at **[Sugarloaf Ridge State Park](http://www.sugarloafpark.org/)**!

Participants will arrive at Sugarloaf Ridge State Park by 8:45 am, with shuttle buses departing at 9:00 am for Hood Mountain Regional Park. The hike begins at the Los Alamos parking lot, taking the Santa Rosa Creek Trail to Homestead Meadow and entering Sugarloaf via the McCormick Addition. The route ascends Quercus Trail, passes the former site of the Grandmother Oak, and stops briefly at Red Hill before continuing up Maple Glen Trail. After lunch atop “Hill 2530,” hikers return to Sugarloaf via ascending Bald Mountain, concluding with a barbecue dinner in the day-use area around 3:30 pm. The journey spans roughly ***11 miles and climbs 3,200 ft in elevation.***

**To register:** https://sec.regfox.com/headwaters-2026
**Cost of ticket:** $130
Ticket includes parking, shuttle transportation to Hood Mountain, snacks, drinks, a commemorative enamel pin, and a BBQ dinner.
Post-hike BBQ dinner, starting at 4:30 pm.

**Ticket sales end on April 5 and may sell out before this date.**

title: Awaken Yoga @ Hotel Petaluma
description:
Nourish 108 Ayurveda & Yoga
Join me for 60 minutes of nourishing morning yoga in the serene Brooklyn Ballroom at the Hotel Petaluma. While I am here to offer a sequence of poses, this practice is yours to give your mind and body what it needs. Please bring your own yoga mat and any props you may need (strap, block, bolster, blanket) and arrive a few minutes early to get situated. Extra mats available for hotel guests.

All levels welcome!

Drop in is $18, class passes available, FREE for hotel guests. Sign up through the calendar at [www.nourish108.com/classes](www.nourish108.com/classes) or hotel front desk if you are a guest. Pay by credit/debit in advance or cash/Venmo on-site.

I look forward to seeing you on your mat!

title: Butter & Egg Days Parade & Festival 2026
location: Petaluma, CA
description: Theme: "America's Birthday - Petaluma Style" Celebrating 250 years of independence, ingenuity, and small-town pride. “Americas’ Birthday – Petaluma Style” celebrates the nation’s 250th Birthday by honoring Petaluma’s deep agricultural roots and hometown spirit. This year’s Butter & Egg Days Parade recognizes the farmers, families, immigrants, makers, entrepreneurs, and creatives whose hard work, imagination, and ingenuity reflect the enduring American Dream. This celebration also pays respect to the Indigenous peoples who first stewarded this land, the courage of our founders, and the dedication of the dairy and egg farming community that shaped our town. Rooted in community, innovation, and independence, the parade celebrates the small-town spirit that continues to make Petaluma a vibrant place to call home. Head to Downtown Petaluma and celebrate with our community on this day of fun, history, and local pride! Parade participants incorporate the theme “America's Birthday - Petaluma S

title: Butter & Egg Days Parade and Festival
location: Downtown Petaluma,20 4th St, Petaluma, CA 94952, USA
description:
2026 Annual Butter & Egg Days Parade and Festival

Date: Saturday, April 18, 2026

Step into Petaluma's flowering world of curiosity and delight!

The 2026 Butter & Egg Days Parade will take place Saturday, April 18. Participants and spectators alike to celebrate the magic and charm of our historic town. Colorful floats, costumed participants, and lively performances will celebrate Petaluma’s rich history and agricultural heritage. Join in a day of fun and local pride that promises to delight visitors of all ages!

The Butter & Egg Days festivities kick off on Saturday, April 11 with the Opening Ceremonies at the Petaluma Historical Library and Museum (20 Fourth St), from Noon to 1pm. Mayor Kevin McDonnell will read a proclamation honoring the Grand Marshal and Good Egg. Come enjoy this display that shows the heart of our community.

One week later on Saturday, April 18, the Butter & Egg Days Parade steps off at noon and will march through the historic downtown. The parade will showcase over 100 units with approximately 3,000 participants and 200 volunteers. Clover Sonoma will return to lead the Clover Kids’ Parade, which precedes the main parade at 11:30am with a procession of preschoolers and primary grade youngsters and their families.

The parade is surrounded by a Festival that spreads over four city blocks from 10am-5pm offering a wide variety of food, crafts, beverage gardens, two large kid areas, live music, and more! This annual event aims to bring together the community for a day of fun and entertainment.

The following weekend, Downtown Petaluma continues the celebration with our Annual Antique Faire, on Sunday, April 26.

Visit ButterAndEggDays.com http://www.ButterAndEggDays.com for more information.

Happy Butter & Egg Days!!

title: 945 after Butter n Eggs Parade
location: The Big Easy, 128 American Alley, Petaluma, CA, 94952, United States
description:
Meet 945—a band with alt-rock roots, bold emotional voice, and a live show that hits both heart and floor. Emerging from Sonoma County’s rock scene, they merge the raw energy of 1990s/2000s alt rock with modern ideas and a hauntingly strong female lead vocal. 
Lead singer (raised in Petaluma), Sophia’s voice is one of the band’s core charms—dynamic, raw, honest, capable of whisper-quiet emotion and full-tilt rocker power. Backed by stellar instrumentation and songwriting that deals in real life, 945 delivers originals and reinterpretations that stay with you. 
Today’s show will feel like you’re front-row for a band on the brink; fun, powerful, and unafraid to hold you. Rock favorites, fresh originals, and that kind of performance that echoes well past the set-end. 
Why You’ve Gotta Be Here after the parade: 
Alt rock with soul—fierce, real, and moving 
A band already earning accolades (Best Rock Band for 2 years in a row and best lead vocals for 3 years in a row through Bohemian Magazine)

title: The Big Easy Presents: Studio 54 Revisited
location: The Big Easy, 128 American Alley, Petaluma, CA, 94952, United States
description:
STUDIO 54 Revisited: Marin County’s ultimate disco dance experience returns on Saturday, April 18th at The Big Easy (Petaluma). Studio 54 Revisited brings live energy and groove with the greatest dance hits from the ’70s and beyond. You will hear classics from Chic, KC and the Sunshine Band, Earth, Wind & Fire, Kool & the Gang, the Jackson 5, Prince, and many more songs that keep feet moving and hearts smiling. 
(Featuring professional Bay Area players who know how to turn a night into a celebration. Angie Maserati leads with dynamic vocals, supported by Russ Gold on drums, Aaron Chioino on guitar, Tom Griesser on sax, Geoff Vaughan on bass, and Mickey Wray on keyboards. This group transforms timeless tunes into a live party that makes the crowd feel like they’ve stepped into the dance floor of a legendary disco.) 
Come dressed in your best 70s style to make the night even more immersive. Think wide collars, bold patterns, platform shoes and glitter. Get ready to boogie, sweat, and relive the magic of the disco era as Studio 54 Revisited delivers a set built for dancing and joy.

title: Join us for Guided Buddhist Meditation and time for questions
description:
Meditate with a Monk in Sonoma County
New and experienced meditators are welcome. We will start with direct guidance to begin the practice and allow time to experience the silence of meditation on your own. There will be time at the end for your questions regarding meditation practice.

You may stay on for a potluck meal at 11:00 am. Welcome to bring a dish to share.

This is followed by Sutta Study program from 1:00 to 2:30 pm.

We meet outdoors in the Dhammadharini Monastery Garden in the Sala Pavillion, as long as conducive weather lasts.

Come in person, or register at [here](https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2) to attend online

title: Mindfulness Sunday Meditation: Cultivating Consciousness
description:
Meditate with a Monk in Sonoma County
Join us for a transformative workshop on mindfulness meditation and cultivating consciousness. Dive deep into the teachings of Theravada Buddhism, focusing on insight meditation to develop a clear understanding of the nature of the mind and consciousness. This workshop will include guided meditation sessions to help you strengthen your meditation practice and cultivate mindfulness in your daily life. Whether you are a beginner or an experienced meditator, this workshop offers a welcoming space to explore the depths of your own consciousness and enhance your spiritual journey. Come together with like-minded individuals to deepen your practice and connect with the wisdom of Theravada Buddhism, and provide an opportunity to deepen mindfulness, wisdom, and inner stillness.
Our sessions are led by Bhikkhunis closely associated with our Dhammadharini Sangha. Check out the [recording from the past event](https://youtube.com/live/s07U2kzWoWk?feature=share) on our YouTube channel.
You’re also welcome to join us in person at Dhammadharini Monastery, in the peaceful setting of our outdoor Dana Sala Pavilion
Register for this event on Dhammadharini's website to receive the address:
[https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2](https://www.dhammadharini.net/event-details/sunday-morning-guided-meditation-questions-2)
Check out the sangha website at
[https://www.dhammadharini.net](https://www.dhammadharini.net/)

title: English Country Dance Sebastopol
description:
North Bay Contra Dance
English country dancing is done in long lines (like contra), circles, or in geometric sets (squares, triangles). Like our contra dances, there is live music and a caller. You can come by yourself or with friends, since we change partners after each dance. If you’ve ever done contra or square dancing, much of our dance vocabulary will be familiar to you since both of those styles evolved from English country dancing.

The dance is held the 1st and 3rd Sundays of each month, 2-4:30 pm, to live music, all levels of experience welcome; and occasional 5th Sundays for Advanced dancers. Admission is $15 for members of NBCDS and its affiliates, $8 for young adults ages 19-30, and $3 for youth ages 6 – 18.

description: We are excited to share that the Social Work and Human Services program is hosting a free training on Trauma-Informed Care and Workplace Wellbeing, presented by Hanna Center, for our SWHS students and the broader campus community!  Date: April 21st, 2026  Time: 3:00–5:00 PM  Location: SRJC Student Activity Center (SAC)  This 2-hour interactive session will explore the principles of Trauma-Informed Care and their application in professional environments. title: Tuesday Night Fitness Hike: 5+ Fast Miles, Downtown Petaluma and Western Hills
description:
"SONOMA COUNTY OUTDOORS"
WARNING: THE TUESDAY NIGHT FITNESS HIKE IS AN AEROBIC, FAST-PACED URBAN HIKE! WE TARGET A PACE OF 16-MINUTES PER MILE WITH ONLY BRIEF STOPS (TO REGROUP AS NEEDED). OUR SUMMER ROUTE INCLUDES THE HILLS IN HELEN PUTNAM PARK (740 FT. ELEVATION GAIN), AND OUR WINTER ROUTE EXPLORES THE WATER TANKS (690 FT. ELEVATION GAIN). IF YOU FIND YOU ARE HAVING DIFFICULTY KEEPING UP WITH THE LEADERS IN THIS GROUP, PLEASE USE THE OPT-OUT POINT AT THE FIRST MILE OF THE HIKE! ANOTHER OPTION IS OUR WEDNESDAY NIGHT WALK FOR A MORE MODERATE PACE. THANK YOU FOR HELPING ME KEEP THIS HIKE AN AEROBIC WORKOUT!

NOTE:
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike is posted on the SCO Calendar every week. Steve Tubbs is shown as the host each week but he might, or might not, show up to the event. This is, more often than not, a small group of regular, fit walkers who show up on time every Tuesday night to enjoy this rigorous walk. We leave promptly at 6:10 pm from the Kentucky Street entrance to the LanMart Building. So, if you don't see Steve on the Tuesday night you are there, you might see him on the next one.

Tuesday Night Fitness Hike, 5+ miles fast paced urban hike:

This Fitness Hike includes multiple steep uphill portions in Helen Putnam Park (during Daylight Savings Time), and at the Petaluma Water Tanks (after Daylight Savings Time ends).

Tuesday Night Fitness Hike:

* Warning! This is a FAST hike... a real workout!
* 5+ miles
* Meetup time 6:00 pm (We hike at 6:10 sharp)
* Difficulty is STRENUOUS!
* 90 minutes

This is a fast, evening Fitness Hike that starts downtown outside (on the sidewalk) of the LanMart Building (Brixx Restaurant, Heebe Jeebe) on Kentucky Street. We head west to Helen Putnam Park (or the Petaluma Water Tanks when DST ends) for some beautiful vistas before returning to our starting point on Kentucky Street. This hike is generally the same route and is focused primarily on fitness.

WHAT TO BRING:

* A good pair of walking/hiking shoes
* Water (you'll be glad you did)
* A fun attitude!

WHAT ABOUT THE DARKNESS?
Bring a SMALL flashlight. The sidewalks can be treacherous in west Petaluma, and you'll want to see those crags, cracks and upheavals in the concrete. Reflective clothing is also recommended so that vehicles can see you!

SO HOW FAST ARE WE WALKING?
The Wednesday Night Walk is about 2.5 mph (22 - 23 minute miles)
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike nearly 3.5 - 4.0 mph (we target a 16 minute mile, with multiple hills!)

HOW DO YOU PARTICIPATE?
Sign up with Meetup and RSVP Today! This also gets you on our email list to make you aware of upcoming events, hikes and walks.

WHY DO YOU NEED TO RSVP?

Please take the time to RSVP YES when you plan on attending an event. It is equally important to keep your RSVP up to date. If you've RSVP'd YES for an event, we are going to wait for you until the departure time. We leave promptly at 6:10 PM, so if you arrive late you can join us along the route. We typically hike the same route for each hike, and our focus is on aerobic exercise.

QUESTIONS?
Email me with any questions by using the "Contact Us" link on the left hand side bar of this meetup group page.

Hope to see you on a walk or hike soon!

SCO Organizers

title: Figure Drawing Night at Suite G Studio!
description:
Petaluma Figure Drawing Meetup Group
Join us for an evening of creativity and inspiration at the Petaluma Figure Drawing Meetup Group's Figure Drawing Night at Suite G Studio! Whether you're a seasoned artist or just starting out, this event is the perfect opportunity to hone your skills, meet fellow artists, and immerse yourself in the world of figurative painting. Our life drawing sessions are open to artists and art lovers of all levels, so don't be shy to join us and express your creativity on paper. We provide a supportive and encouraging environment where everyone can feel comfortable exploring their artistic talents.

[BECOME A MEMBER](https://www.suitegstudio.com/artist-resources) of Suite G Studio for $40/month. Or RSVP by sending $25 to @suiteg on Venmo or email [suitegstudio620@gmail.com](mailto:suitegstudio620@gmail.com)

Be prepared for an evening filled with sketching, painting, and drawing as we focus on capturing the human form in all its beauty and complexity. This event is a great opportunity to practice your figure drawing techniques and unleash your artistic potential. Whether you prefer using pencils, charcoal, pastels, or paint, there's something for everyone at our Figure Drawing Night. Come and be a part of our community of artists dedicated to celebrating and expressing the beauty of life through art.

title: Wednesday Night Walk: Petaluma Downtown & Riverfront
description:
"SONOMA COUNTY OUTDOORS"
Wednesday Night Walk: Petaluma Downtown and Riverfront

* Want to walk for fitness and exercise?
* New to walking and want to build up stamina?
* Like a slower pace than our Tuesday Night Fitness Hike?
* Would walking with a committed group of walkers help you?

If you said yes to any of these, then the Wednesday Night Walk in Petaluma is for you. We start at Aqus Cafe and take in the West Side of town, including some of Downtown, the Waterfront and a few of the Historic home of old Petaluma. We walk about 3-1/2 to 4-1/2 miles depending on weather conditions and the pace of the group that night.

Afterward if you have the time, join us at Aqus Cafe for a bite to eat and some liquid refreshment before heading home. Aqus Cafe is a true gem of a gathering place, restaurant, coffee house, and live music venue. But it's the people on either side of the counter that make this place so special.

DETAILS:

* Meetup time: 6:00pm (we hike at 6:10 sharp)
* Meetup location: Aqus Cafe
* Distance: 3.5 to 4-1/2 miles
* Duration: 75-80 minutes
* Pace: Easy/Moderate (about 2.5-3 mph or 20-24 minute miles)
* Difficulty: Easy (if you've walked 3-4 miles recently)
* Hills: Mostly flat with a few small and easy to climb hills
* Optional After-Hike Gathering at Aqus Cafe

WHAT TO BRING:

* A good pair of walking shoes
* Water (you'll be glad you did)
* A fun attitude!

SO HOW FAST ARE WE WALKING?
The Wednesday Night Walk is about 2.5 - 3 mph (20-24 minute miles)
The Tuesday Night Fitness Hike is about 3.5-4 mph (15-17 minute miles)

HOW DO YOU PARTICIPATE?
Sign up with Meetup and RSVP Today! This also gets you on our email list to make you aware of upcoming events, hikes and walks. You will not be spammed. If you are, let me know. Spamming will not be tolerated.

WHY DO YOU NEED TO RSVP?
It's important to RSVP for these walks. When all the people have arrived for a walk that have RSVP'd, we leave... it might mean we leave early. If you didn't RSVP we are not going to wait for you. Plus, it's just a nice courtesy to the organizer and other participants. Please take the time to RSVP.

QUESTIONS?
Email me with any questions by using the "Contact Us" link on the left hand side bar of this meetup group page.

Hope to see you on a walk or hike soon!

Gary
SCO Organizer
